topics: Hrs. 30 Min. Prerequisites (if any) None Course Type Lab Course Objectives: 1. To summarize the effect of temperature on the viscosity of hydrocarbon liquids 2. To test the quality and hence the acceptability of DCP and AFFF in fighting fire. 3. To test the performance and hence the acceptability of portable fire extinguishers in fighting fire of Class A B C D and Electrical Fire Hazards. 4. To explain the effect of temperature on the strength of building materials. Expt. No. Experiments 1 Determination of flash Point of hydrocarbon liquids. 2 Determination of Fire Point and Pour Point of hydrocarbon liquids. 3 Study on the effect of temperature on the viscosity of hydrocarbon liquids using redwood viscometer. 4 Physical tests on Dry Chemical Powder as per relevant Indian standard specifications 5 Physical tests on AFFF as per relevant Indian standard specifications 6 Performance Tests on Portable Fire Extinguishers - AFFF

Water and DCP type 7 Determination of flame spread rate of materials 8 Study on the effect of temperature on strength of building materials - Concrete and masonry units. 9 Rebound hammer test on concrete exposed to elevated temperatures. 10 Test of non-combustibility of Building Materials. 11 Study on Class D Fire Extinguisher. 12 Study of Extinguishers in Electrical Fire. Course Assessment Method (CIE: 50 marks
ESE: 50 marks) Continuous Internal Evaluation Marks (CIE): Attendance Preparation/Pre-Lab Work experiments

Viva and Timely completion of Lab Reports / Record (Continuous Assessment) Internal Examination Total 5 25 20 50 End Semester Examination Marks (ESE): Procedure/ Preparatory work/Design/ Algorithm Conduct of experiment/ Execution of work/ troubleshooting/ Programming Result with valid inference/ Quality of Output Viva voce Record Total 10 15 10 10 5 50  Submission of Record: Students shall be allowed for the end semester examination only upon submitting the duly certified record.  Endorsement by External Examiner: The external examiner shall endorse the record Course Outcomes (COs) At the end of the course students should be able to: Course Outcome Bloom’s Knowledge Level (KL) CO1 Summarize the effect of temperature on the viscosity of hydrocarbon liquids K3 CO2 Test the quality and hence the acceptability of DCP and AFFF in fighting fire. K3 CO3 Test the performance and hence the acceptability of portable fire extinguishers in fighting fire of Class A B C D and Electrical Fire Hazards. K3 CO4 Explain the effect of temperature on the strength of building materials K2 CO5 Interpret the method of determination of the flame spread rate of materials. K4 CO6 Explain the method of determination of the combustibility of building materials. K2 Note: K1- Remember

Preparation and Pre-Lab Work (7 Marks)  Pre-Lab Assignments: Assessment of pre-lab assignments or quizzes that test understanding of the upcoming experiment.  Understanding of Theory: Evaluation based on students’ preparation and understanding of the theoretical background related to the experiments. 2. Conduct of Experiments (7 Marks)  Procedure and Execution: Adherence to correct procedures
accurate execution of experiments
and following safety protocols.  Skill Proficiency: Proficiency in handling equipment
accuracy in observations
and troubleshooting skills during the experiments.  Teamwork: Collaboration and participation in group experiments. 3. Lab Reports and Record Keeping (6 Marks)  Quality of Reports: Clarity
completeness and accuracy of lab reports. Proper documentation of experiments

data analysis and conclusions.  Timely Submission: Adhering to deadlines for submitting lab reports/rough record and maintaining a well-organized fair record. 4. Viva Voce (5 Marks)  Oral Examination: Ability to explain the experiment
results and underlying principles during a viva voce session. Final Marks Averaging: The final marks for preparation
conduct of experiments
viva

and record are the average of all the specified experiments in the syllabus. Evaluation Pattern for End Semester Examination (50 Marks) 1. Procedure/Preliminary Work/Design/Algorithm (10 Marks)  Procedure Understanding and Description: Clarity in explaining the procedure and understanding each step involved.  Preliminary Work and Planning: Thoroughness in planning and organizing materials/equipment.  Algorithm Development: Correctness and efficiency of the algorithm related to the experiment.  Creativity and logic in algorithm or experimental design. 2. Conduct of Experiment/Execution of Work/Programming (15 Marks)  Setup and Execution: Proper setup and accurate execution of the experiment or programming task. 3. Result with Valid Inference/Quality of Output (10 Marks)  Accuracy of Results: Precision and correctness of the obtained results.  Analysis and Interpretation: Validity of inferences drawn from the experiment or quality of program output. 4. Viva Voce (10 Marks)  Ability to explain the experiment
procedure results and answer related questions  Proficiency in answering questions related to theoretical and practical aspects of the subject. 5. Record (5 Marks)  Completeness
clarity
and accuracy of the lab record submitted SEMESTER S5 CHEMICAL ENGINEERING LAB
